mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects broad economic strength and corporate pricing power. A positive 3-month change in INDPRO signals higher expected earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for more than four consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate is a primary indicator of a recession, signaling a sharp contraction in demand and corporate profits. A rise of more than 0.3 percentage points in UNRATE over 3 months triggers a negative outlook for equities.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a high policy rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'stagflationary' environment. This combination compresses corporate margins and increases the discount rate on future earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation and high rates were characteristic of the poor equity returns in the 1970s.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting SPX when the trigger is active fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts is a leading indicator of a broad-based economic slowdown, as housing has a large multiplier effect. This effect is amplified during a monetary tightening cycle, as high rates directly impact mortgage affordability and construction financing.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness preceded several US recessions.
failure_condition: The signal shows no significant negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.10) during periods where FEDFUNDS rate is above its 3-year moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues. This positive surprise drives short-term positive equity returns as analysts update earnings forecasts.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a top-quartile monthly RSAFS release is not statistically different from the average return following a bottom-quartile release.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a healthy, expanding economy. This reduces recession fears and supports the outlook for corporate earnings, leading to positive equity market performance over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly employment report is a key market-moving data release.
failure_condition: A rolling 36-month beta of 20-day forward S&P 500 returns to the 3-month change in PAYEMS turns negative for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, it significantly increases the cost of capital for businesses and weighs on valuations by raising the discount rate. A FEDFUNDS rate above 5% acts as a persistent headwind for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2000 and 2007 market peaks were preceded by FEDFUNDS rates above 5%.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is above 5% is not statistically lower than when it is below 3% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of contracting industrial production (INDPRO) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) is a powerful, mutually-reinforcing signal of a cyclical downturn. This joint signal of weakness in both goods production and the labor market presages a broad decline in corporate earnings and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series are key components of The Conference Board's Coincident Economic Index.
failure_condition: The Sharpe ratio of a strategy shorting the S&P 500 on this signal is negative over any 5-year rolling window.
